name: Muslim American Youth Foundation
mission: Our mission is to educate and instil a drive for personal academic success, enrich through experience and expansion of what is really possible, and enlighten by means of taking the aforementioned experiences and incorporating them into their personal lives, households and communities.
theoryOfChange: By providing culturally relevant and spiritually grounded education, sports, and mentoring (inputs), MAYF aims to strengthen identity and values (outputs) to empower youth to navigate modern challenges and lead their communities (outcomes).
scoreSummary: Muslim American Youth Foundation shows strong impact and good alignment.

evaluationSources: HEAL Capacity Grant (Washington State Department of Health (DOH))

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 5.7% of revenue ($118,688 on $2,087,958)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue <$5M, CEO pay above 5% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions financial health and program expense ratio but does not mention the governance concerns (CEO compensation) which negatively impacted the score.
Website shows related EIN (null), likely parent/subsidiary of 81-3135852

risks.description: CEO compensation is 5.7% of revenue ($118,688 on $2,087,958)

score_details.score_summary: Muslim American Youth Foundation shows strong impact and good alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: No explicit mention of zakat donations found on the official website or related US-specific charity profiles for Muslim American Youth Foundation. While a 'Muslim Youth Foundation' (MYF) in the UK explicitly mentions accepting zakat, this is a different entity and does not apply to the US-based Muslim American Youth Foundation. (Source: https://mayf.org)

all_citations.claim: The organization holds 13.4 months of working capital and a program expense ratio of 72.1%.
The Muslim American Youth Foundation reported a total revenue of $2,087,958 and a fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised.
The foundation's primary programs include community services, youth sports, athletics, and mentoring.
Donors can support the organization's mission through various giving methods.
The organization offers volunteer opportunities for community engagement and youth support.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its solid financial efficiency and strong working capital, though its impact score is tempered by a focus on capacity building rather than direct poverty alleviation.
areas_for_improvement: Enhance transparency regarding specific program outcomes and impact metrics
Increase the program expense ratio to direct a higher percentage of funds toward direct services

dimension_explanations.alignment: MAYF shows medium alignment for Muslim donors seeking to support domestic youth causes through sadaqah [4]. The organization addresses the urgent need for community-based youth mentoring and athletics, though it lacks the high-urgency profile associated with international relief efforts [3][5].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates a high level of financial health, maintaining 13.4 months of working capital and a program expense ratio of 72.1% [1][2]. While the organization successfully maintains its facility and programs, the impact is categorized as capacity building, focusing on long-term youth enrichment rather than immediate crisis intervention [3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim American Youth Foundation provides a dedicated facility for youth education, enrichment, and athletic mentoring to empower the next generation.
strengths: High fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.02 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 13.4 months of working capital
Dedicated focus on domestic youth enrichment and community services
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Youth Foundation (MAYF) operates as a community hub focused on youth enlightenment through educational programs and sports mentoring [1][3]. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $2,087,958 to support its mission of creating a safe space for youth development [2]. Supporters can contribute to these initiatives through various sadaqah-eligible donation methods or by engaging in volunteer opportunities [4][5].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its solid financial efficiency and strong working capital, though its impact score is tempered by a focus on capacity building rather than direct poverty alleviation.

areas_for_improvement.context: The current <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 72.1%</cite> is lower than the industry benchmark for high-performing charities, suggesting room to optimize spending toward direct services.
There is currently <cite id="16">no specific beneficiary data available</cite> to calculate cost-per-beneficiary metrics, which limits the ability to assess direct impact effectiveness.

bbb_assessment.summary: This organization has not yet been evaluated against BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's high growth rate and grantmaking activity may explain some of the overhead costs associated with scaling operations.
risk_factors: <cite id="23">CEO compensation of $91,000</cite> represents over 5% of total revenue, which may warrant scrutiny for an organization of this size.
<cite id="7">72.1% of expenses</cite> are directed toward non-programmatic costs.
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="7">moderate program spending</cite> and executive compensation levels relative to revenue.

confidence.notes: Strong financial trend data but limited impact metrics.

alignment.explanation: As a <cite id="20">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite> organization, it aligns well with donors interested in <cite id="21">domestic poverty and youth development</cite> within a Muslim-led framework.
alignment.improvement: The foundation could improve alignment for faith-driven donors by <cite id="22">clearly stating the status of its programs</cite> regarding specific charitable categories.

credibility.explanation: Donors can find confidence in the organization's <cite id="14">100% independent board</cite> and its <cite id="17">documented theory of change</cite>. However, credibility is currently limited by the <cite id="18">lack of a Candid transparency seal</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should seek a Candid Seal of Transparency and publish its full audited financial statements.

impact.explanation: The foundation operates with a <cite id="7">72.1% program ratio</cite> and focuses on capacity building. While it has <cite id="19">verified evidence and outcomes</cite>, the lack of beneficiary counts prevents a full efficiency analysis.
impact.improvement: The organization should <cite id="16">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> so that cost-effectiveness can be accurately assessed by donors.

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports other domestic humanitarian and relief organizations to extend community reach.

rich_narrative.headline: The Muslim American Youth Foundation provides a dedicated facility for youth education, enrichment, and athletic mentoring to empower the next generation.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to invest in the long-term sustainability of Muslim youth infrastructure in the United States through an organization with high financial reserves.
donor_motivations: Value domestic community building and youth mentorship
Prefer supporting organizations with strong growth trajectories
Want to fund a hub that also supports other local charities through grants
Prioritize financial stability and low fundraising costs
giving_considerations: A significant portion of the budget is held in reserves for future facility needs
Impact is measured through capacity building rather than individual beneficiary metrics
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those who prioritize charities with program expense ratios above 90%.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects <cite id="19">verified evidence and outcomes</cite> and a strong theory of change, though it lacks specific beneficiary-level cost data.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation creates impact by providing a physical facility that hosts educational, athletic, and health programs, thereby fostering a safe environment for youth development and community resilience.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the facility-based model is effectively translating into positive youth development outcomes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="24">$2.1M revenue level</cite> and a broad mission, the organization has a significant funding gap to expand its facility-based services.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="11">exceptionally high working capital</cite> and its role as a <cite id="9">significant grantmaker</cite> to other humanitarian groups.

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="8">$0.03 to raise every $1</cite>, ensuring that donor acquisition costs remain remarkably low.
MAYF maintains <cite id="11">13.4 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant safety net for long-term operational stability.
The foundation has achieved a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 73.3%</cite>, indicating rapidly expanding community support and capacity.

strengths_deep_dive: The Muslim American Youth Foundation demonstrates a high degree of financial resilience, characterized by <cite id="12">total assets of $5,337,942</cite> against <cite id="13">liabilities of $3,638,113</cite>. This strong balance sheet is complemented by an <cite id="14">independent board with 100% membership</cite> not receiving compensation from the organization, which enhances governance oversight. Furthermore, the organization has successfully integrated <cite id="15">health advocacy and athletics</cite> into its core mission, creating a holistic approach to youth enrichment that resonates with domestic donors.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Youth Foundation (MAYF) serves as a vital community hub dedicated to <cite id="1">youth education, enrichment, and athletic mentoring</cite>. Based in the United States, the organization focuses on empowering the next generation by providing a safe facility for <cite id="2">community services and faith-based programs</cite>. In the 2023 fiscal year, MAYF demonstrated significant growth, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $1,734,105</cite>, which represents a remarkable <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 73.3%</cite>. The foundation's operations are diversified, drawing <cite id="5">$1,502,397 from contributions</cite> and <cite id="6">$225,128 from program service revenue</cite>. While the organization maintains a <cite id="7">program expense ratio of 72.1%</cite>, it exhibits exceptional <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency at $0.02 per $1 raised</cite>. Beyond its own facility-based programming, MAYF acts as a community supporter, distributing <cite id="9">$174,234 in total grants</cite> to other domestic organizations, including the <cite id="10">Humanitarian African Relief Organization and Amoud Foundation</cite>. This dual role as both a direct service provider and a grantmaker allows MAYF to address broader humanitarian needs while maintaining its core focus on domestic youth development.
